---
title: "SLAM Metabolic Class ITP treatment response"
subtitle: "Cohort 2005, predicted from Cohorts 2010, 2011, 2015, 2016"
author: "William Mueller, Joshua Preston"
data: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%Y %B %d')`"
output:
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
--- 

```{r setup, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
library(consoler)
library(SLAM)
library(survival)
library(flextable)
library(broom)

set.seed(365)

# setting global knitr options for r markdown output
knitr::opts_chunk$set(autodep = TRUE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(warning = FALSE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(message = FALSE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(cache = FALSE)
```

# Load in input data

```{r read csv}
surv <- read.csv("../00a_itp2/output/itp_tx_control_surv.csv")
surv <- surv[, c("idno", "le_wk", "dead_censor")]

predicted <- read.csv("../04_create_census/output/itp_controls_p_treatment/test_census.csv")
predicted_keep <- c(
  "idno",
  "sex",
  "sex_M",
  "sex_F",
  "cohort",
  "site",
  "le_wk",
  "dead_censor",
  "prob1",
  "prob2",
  "prob3",
  "new_class_bw"
)
predicted <- predicted[, predicted_keep]

tx_info <- read.csv("../00a_itp2/output/itp_tx_control_test.csv")
tx_info <- tx_info[, c("idno", "tx")]
tx_info <- tx_info[!duplicated(tx_info$idno), ]
```

# Merge predicted and tx response

```{r merge}
predicted <- merge(tx_info, predicted, by = "idno", all.x = TRUE)
predicted$tx_cohort <- paste0(predicted$tx, "_", predicted$cohort)

predicted <- predicted[predicted$le_wk > 86, ]
```

# Create tx KM Curves

```{r km creation, fig.height = 6, fig.width = 18}
classes <- sort(unique(predicted$new_class_bw))

plots <- lapply(classes, function(class) {
  index <- predicted$new_class_bw == class
  data <- predicted[index, ]
  plot_km <- SLAM::surv_plot_km(data,
    var = "tx",
    age_death = "le_wk",
    event = "dead_censor",
    title = paste("Predicted Class", class),
    pval = "",
    surv.median.line = "none"
  )
  plot <- plot_km$plot$plot +
    ggplot2::scale_color_manual(
      name = "",
      labels = c("Control", "Rapamycin"),
      values = c("tx=control" = "#0096ff", "tx=rapa" = "#ff5147")
    ) +
    ggplot2::theme_bw() +
    ggplot2::coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 200), ylim = c(0, NA))

  hrs <- plot_km$hr
  list(plot = plot, hr = hrs)
})

hrs <- lapply(plots, `[[`, "hr")
plots <- lapply(plots, `[[`, "plot")


title_theme <- cowplot::ggdraw() +
  cowplot::draw_label("Rapamycin Treatment Response by Predicted Class")

plots_panel <- cowplot::plot_grid(
  plotlist = plots, nrow = 1, byrow = FALSE,
  greedy = FALSE, labels = "AUTO"
)

plots_panel <- cowplot::plot_grid(title_theme, plots_panel,
  rel_heights = c(.05, 1),
  ncol = 1
)
plots_panel
```

### Class by treatment tabulation

```{r create demographic table}
tab <- as.data.frame.matrix(
  table(predicted$tx, predicted$new_class_bw)
)

tab$Treatment <- rownames(tab)
rownames(tab) <- NULL

tab <- tab[, c("Treatment", "1", "2", "3")]
names(tab) <- c("Treatment", "Class 1", "Class 2", "Class 3")

tab$Treatment <- c(
  control = "Control",
  rapa = "Rapamycin"
)[tab$Treatment]

tab$Total <- rowSums(
  tab[, c("Class 1", "Class 2", "Class 3")]
)

# Add a total row
total_row <- data.frame(
  Treatment = "Total",
  `Class 1` = sum(tab$`Class 1`),
  `Class 2` = sum(tab$`Class 2`),
  `Class 3` = sum(tab$`Class 3`),
  Total = sum(tab$Total),
  check.names = FALSE
)

tab <- rbind(tab, total_row)

ft_tx_class <- flextable(tab)

ft_tx_class <- add_header_row(
  ft_tx_class,
  values = c(
    "Treatment",
    "Body-Weight Trajectory Class",
    "Total"
  ),
  colwidths = c(1, 3, 1)
)

ft_tx_class <- set_header_labels(
  ft_tx_class,
  Treatment = "Treatment Group",
  `Class 1` = "Class 1",
  `Class 2` = "Class 2",
  `Class 3` = "Class 3",
  Total = "Total"
)

ft_tx_class <- theme_booktabs(ft_tx_class)

ft_tx_class <- bold(
  ft_tx_class,
  part = "header"
)

ft_tx_class <- bg(
  ft_tx_class,
  part = "header",
  bg = "#D9EAF7"
)

ft_tx_class <- bg(
  ft_tx_class,
  i = 2,
  bg = "#F2F7FB",
  part = "body"
)

# Format the total row
ft_tx_class <- bold(
  ft_tx_class,
  i = nrow(tab),
  part = "body"
)

ft_tx_class <- bg(
  ft_tx_class,
  i = nrow(tab),
  bg = "#D9EAF7",
  part = "body"
)

ft_tx_class <- hline(
  ft_tx_class,
  i = nrow(tab) - 1,
  border = officer::fp_border(
    color = "black",
    width = 1.5
  ),
  part = "body"
)

ft_tx_class <- align(
  ft_tx_class,
  j = "Treatment",
  align = "left",
  part = "all"
)

ft_tx_class <- align(
  ft_tx_class,
  j = c("Class 1", "Class 2", "Class 3", "Total"),
  align = "center",
  part = "all"
)

ft_tx_class <- bold(
  ft_tx_class,
  j = "Total",
  part = "body"
)

ft_tx_class <- font(
  ft_tx_class,
  fontname = "Arial",
  part = "all"
)

ft_tx_class <- fontsize(
  ft_tx_class,
  size = 10,
  part = "body"
)

ft_tx_class <- fontsize(
  ft_tx_class,
  size = 10.5,
  part = "header"
)

ft_tx_class <- padding(
  ft_tx_class,
  padding = 6,
  part = "all"
)

ft_tx_class <- autofit(ft_tx_class)

ft_tx_class <- set_caption(
  ft_tx_class,
  caption = paste(
    "Distribution of Body-Weight Trajectory Classes",
    "by Treatment Group"
  )
)

ft_tx_class <- add_footer_lines(
  ft_tx_class,
  values = paste(
    "Values represent the number of animals assigned",
    "to each latent trajectory class."
  )
)

ft_tx_class <- italic(
  ft_tx_class,
  part = "footer"
)

ft_tx_class <- fontsize(
  ft_tx_class,
  size = 8.5,
  part = "footer"
)

ft_tx_class
```

```{r save flextable class}
dir.create(file.path("output", "tables"), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
flextable::save_as_image(
  x = ft_tx_class,
  path = file.path("output", "tables", "body_weight_class_by_treatment.png"),
  res = 300,
  expand = 20
)
```

```{r save hrs }
hrs_table <- lapply(
  hrs,
  function(table) {
    # Keep the numeric columns alongside `final`. surv_gethr computes
    # value/lower/upper/pval; only the pre-formatted string used to survive,
    # which left downstream unable to apply its own significance thresholds
    # (SLAM bakes in p<0.005 for "**", not the conventional 0.01).
    # `final` stays first so anything indexing column 1 is unaffected.
    keep <- intersect(c("final", "value", "lower", "upper", "pval"),
                      colnames(table$hr_table))
    table <- table$hr_table[, keep, drop = FALSE]
    table
  }
)
names(hrs_table) <- classes

hrs_table <- lapply(names(hrs_table), function(name) {
  table <- hrs_table[[name]]
  table <- cbind(Class = name, Treatment = rownames(table), table)
  rownames(table) <- NULL
  table
})


output_path <- file.path("output/hr_table")
if (!dir.exists(output_path)) {
  dir.create(output_path, recursive = TRUE)
}
save(hrs_table, file = file.path(output_path, "hr_table.RDATA"))
```

```{r save dose plots}
for (i in seq_along(plots)) {
  plot <- plots[[i]]
  name <- paste0("plot_", i, ".png")
  output_path <- file.path("output", "treatment_response")
  dir.create(output_path, recursive = TRUE)
  output_path <- normalizePath(output_path)
  ggplot2::ggsave(
    filename = name, plot = plot, device = "png", path = output_path,
    width = 6,
    height = 6
  )
}
```

```{r print hrs, results = 'asis'}
for (table in hrs_table) {
  print(kableExtra::kable(table))
}
```

### Combined class 1 and 3 to make nonresponder group and increase power

```{r combine class 1 and 3, fig.height = 6, fig.width = 12}
predicted$nonresponder <- ifelse(predicted$new_class_bw == 3, 1,
  ifelse(predicted$new_class_bw == 1, 1, 0)
)

table(predicted$nonresponder)

classes <- sort(unique(predicted$nonresponder))

plots <- lapply(classes, function(class) {
  index <- predicted$nonresponder == class
  data <- predicted[index, ]
  plot_km <- SLAM::surv_plot_km(data,
    var = "tx",
    age_death = "le_wk",
    event = "dead_censor",
    # class 0 = responder group; class 1 = predicted classes 1 and 3 pooled
    # (see the ifelse above). These are the published titles -- the combined
    # curve is figure 5H.
    title = if (class == 0) "Responder" else "Predicted Classes 1+3",
    pval = "",
    surv.median.line = "none"
  )
  plot <- plot_km$plot$plot +
    ggplot2::scale_color_manual(
      name = "",
      labels = c("Control", "Rapamycin"),
      values = c("tx=control" = "#0096ff", "tx=rapa" = "#ff5147")
    ) +
    ggplot2::theme_bw() +
    ggplot2::coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 200), ylim = c(0, NA))

  hrs <- plot_km$hr
  list(plot = plot, hr = hrs)
})

hrs <- lapply(plots, `[[`, "hr")
plots <- lapply(plots, `[[`, "plot")


title_theme <- cowplot::ggdraw() +
  cowplot::draw_label("Rapamycin Treatment Response by Predicted Class")

plots_panel <- cowplot::plot_grid(
  plotlist = plots, nrow = 1, byrow = FALSE,
  greedy = FALSE, labels = "AUTO"
)

plots_panel <- cowplot::plot_grid(title_theme, plots_panel,
  rel_heights = c(.05, 1),
  ncol = 1
)

plots_panel

# Save the pooled-class KMs individually. They previously existed only as this
# inline panel, so nothing downstream could reference them -- the combined
# "Predicted Classes 1+3" curve is figure 5H.
combined_path <- file.path("output", "treatment_response_combined")
dir.create(combined_path, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
combined_names <- c("responder", "predicted_classes_1_3")
for (i in seq_along(plots)) {
  ggplot2::ggsave(
    filename = paste0(combined_names[[i]], ".png"),
    plot = plots[[i]], device = "png",
    path = normalizePath(combined_path),
    width = 6, height = 6
  )
}
```

### Nonresponder by treatment tabulation

```{r nonresponder flextable}
tab_nr <- as.data.frame.matrix(
  table(predicted$tx, predicted$nonresponder)
)

tab_nr$Treatment <- rownames(tab_nr)
rownames(tab_nr) <- NULL

tab_nr <- tab_nr[, c("Treatment", "0", "1")]

names(tab_nr) <- c(
  "Treatment",
  "Responder",
  "Nonresponder"
)

tab_nr$Treatment <- c(
  control = "Control",
  rapa = "Rapamycin"
)[tab_nr$Treatment]

tab_nr$Total <- rowSums(
  tab_nr[, c("Responder", "Nonresponder")]
)

# Add total row
total_row_nr <- data.frame(
  Treatment = "Total",
  Responder = sum(tab_nr$Responder),
  Nonresponder = sum(tab_nr$Nonresponder),
  Total = sum(tab_nr$Total),
  check.names = FALSE
)

tab_nr <- rbind(tab_nr, total_row_nr)

ft_tx_nonresponder <- flextable(tab_nr)

ft_tx_nonresponder <- add_header_row(
  ft_tx_nonresponder,
  values = c(
    "Treatment",
    "Response Classification",
    "Total"
  ),
  colwidths = c(1, 2, 1)
)

ft_tx_nonresponder <- set_header_labels(
  ft_tx_nonresponder,
  Treatment = "Treatment Group",
  Responder = "Responder",
  Nonresponder = "Nonresponder",
  Total = "Total"
)

ft_tx_nonresponder <- theme_booktabs(
  ft_tx_nonresponder
)

ft_tx_nonresponder <- bold(
  ft_tx_nonresponder,
  part = "header"
)

ft_tx_nonresponder <- bg(
  ft_tx_nonresponder,
  part = "header",
  bg = "#D9EAF7"
)

ft_tx_nonresponder <- bg(
  ft_tx_nonresponder,
  i = 2,
  bg = "#F2F7FB",
  part = "body"
)

# Format total row
ft_tx_nonresponder <- bold(
  ft_tx_nonresponder,
  i = nrow(tab_nr),
  part = "body"
)

ft_tx_nonresponder <- bg(
  ft_tx_nonresponder,
  i = nrow(tab_nr),
  bg = "#D9EAF7",
  part = "body"
)

ft_tx_nonresponder <- hline(
  ft_tx_nonresponder,
  i = nrow(tab_nr) - 1,
  border = officer::fp_border(
    color = "black",
    width = 1.5
  ),
  part = "body"
)

ft_tx_nonresponder <- align(
  ft_tx_nonresponder,
  j = "Treatment",
  align = "left",
  part = "all"
)

ft_tx_nonresponder <- align(
  ft_tx_nonresponder,
  j = c(
    "Responder",
    "Nonresponder",
    "Total"
  ),
  align = "center",
  part = "all"
)

ft_tx_nonresponder <- bold(
  ft_tx_nonresponder,
  j = "Total",
  part = "body"
)

ft_tx_nonresponder <- font(
  ft_tx_nonresponder,
  fontname = "Arial",
  part = "all"
)

ft_tx_nonresponder <- fontsize(
  ft_tx_nonresponder,
  size = 10,
  part = "body"
)

ft_tx_nonresponder <- fontsize(
  ft_tx_nonresponder,
  size = 10.5,
  part = "header"
)

ft_tx_nonresponder <- padding(
  ft_tx_nonresponder,
  padding = 6,
  part = "all"
)

ft_tx_nonresponder <- autofit(
  ft_tx_nonresponder
)

ft_tx_nonresponder <- set_caption(
  ft_tx_nonresponder,
  caption = paste(
    "Distribution of Treatment Response",
    "by Treatment Group"
  )
)

ft_tx_nonresponder <- add_footer_lines(
  ft_tx_nonresponder,
  values = paste(
    "Values represent the number of animals classified",
    "as responders or nonresponders."
  )
)

ft_tx_nonresponder <- italic(
  ft_tx_nonresponder,
  part = "footer"
)

ft_tx_nonresponder <- fontsize(
  ft_tx_nonresponder,
  size = 8.5,
  part = "footer"
)

ft_tx_nonresponder
```

```{r save nonresponder}
flextable::save_as_image(
  x = ft_tx_nonresponder,
  path = file.path("output", "treatment_response_by_group.png"),
  res = 300,
  expand = 20
)
```

```{r save hrs responder}
hrs_table <- lapply(
  hrs,
  function(table) {
    # Keep the numeric columns alongside `final`. surv_gethr computes
    # value/lower/upper/pval; only the pre-formatted string used to survive,
    # which left downstream unable to apply its own significance thresholds
    # (SLAM bakes in p<0.005 for "**", not the conventional 0.01).
    # `final` stays first so anything indexing column 1 is unaffected.
    keep <- intersect(c("final", "value", "lower", "upper", "pval"),
                      colnames(table$hr_table))
    table <- table$hr_table[, keep, drop = FALSE]
    table
  }
)
names(hrs_table) <- classes

hrs_table <- lapply(names(hrs_table), function(name) {
  table <- hrs_table[[name]]
  table <- cbind(Nonresponder = name, Treatment = rownames(table), table)
  rownames(table) <- NULL
  table
})


output_path <- file.path("output/hr_table_nonresponder")
if (!dir.exists(output_path)) {
  dir.create(output_path, recursive = TRUE)
}
save(hrs_table, file = file.path(output_path, "hr_table_nonresponder.RDATA"))
```

```{r print hrs responder, results = 'asis'}
for (table in hrs_table) {
  print(kableExtra::kable(table))
}
```

### Rapamycin response Cox model (Control Mice Excluded)

```{r cox hazards with nonresponder}
# Cox model -------------------------------------------------------------------

rapa_only <- predicted[predicted$tx == "rapa", ]

cox_rapa <- coxph(
  Surv(le_wk, dead_censor) ~ nonresponder + factor(sex) + factor(site),
  data = rapa_only
)

summary(cox_rapa)


# Extract model results --------------------------------------------------------

cox_table <- broom::tidy(
  cox_rapa,
  exponentiate = TRUE,
  conf.int = TRUE
)

cox_table

# Helper functions -------------------------------------------------------------

fmt_num <- function(x, digits = 2) {
  formatC(as.numeric(x), format = "f", digits = digits)
}

fmt_p <- function(p) {
  ifelse(
    is.na(p),
    "",
    ifelse(p < 0.001, "<0.001", formatC(p, format = "f", digits = 3))
  )
}


# Clean Cox table --------------------------------------------------------------

cox_table_clean <- data.frame(
  Variable = c(
    "Nonresponder",
    "Male sex",
    "Site: UM",
    "Site: UT"
  ),
  HR = fmt_num(cox_table$estimate, 2),
  `95% CI` = paste0(
    fmt_num(cox_table$conf.low, 2),
    "–",
    fmt_num(cox_table$conf.high, 2)
  ),
  `p-value` = fmt_p(cox_table$p.value),
  check.names = FALSE
)

cox_table_clean


# Flextable --------------------------------------------------------------------

ft <- flextable(cox_table_clean)

ft <- theme_booktabs(ft)
ft <- autofit(ft)

ft <- align(ft, align = "center", part = "all")
ft <- align(ft, j = "Variable", align = "left", part = "all")

ft <- bold(ft, part = "header")
ft <- fontsize(ft, size = 10, part = "all")

ft <- add_header_lines(
  ft,
  values = "Association Between Rapamycin Nonresponse and Survival"
)

ft <- add_footer_lines(
  ft,
  values = paste0(
    "Cox proportional hazards model restricted to rapamycin-treated animals. ",
    "Model adjusted for sex and site. HR > 1 indicates increased mortality risk. ",
    "n = ", nrow(rapa_only), "; events = ", sum(rapa_only$dead_censor == 1, na.rm = TRUE), "."
  )
)

ft <- align(ft, i = 1, align = "center", part = "header")
ft <- bold(ft, i = 1, part = "header")

ft
```

### Repeated downsampling sensitivity analysis

```{r downsample rapamycin}
# Keep only Class 2
predicted_2 <- predicted[predicted$new_class_bw == 2, ]

# Make sure treatment reference is control
predicted_2$tx <- factor(predicted_2$tx, levels = c("control", "rapa"))

n_downsample <- 85
n_iter <- 1000

fit_downsample_hr <- function(data, n = 85) {
  sampled_index <- sample(seq_len(nrow(data)), size = n, replace = FALSE)
  sampled_data <- data[sampled_index, ]

  fit <- coxph(
    Surv(le_wk, dead_censor) ~ tx,
    data = sampled_data
  )

  s <- summary(fit)

  data.frame(
    log_hr = coef(fit)[["txrapa"]],
    hr = exp(coef(fit)[["txrapa"]]),
    lower = s$conf.int["txrapa", "lower .95"],
    upper = s$conf.int["txrapa", "upper .95"],
    p_value = s$coefficients["txrapa", "Pr(>|z|)"],
    n = nrow(sampled_data),
    events = sum(sampled_data$dead_censor == 1),
    row.names = NULL
  )
}

downsample_hrs <- lapply(seq_len(n_iter), function(i) {
  out <- fit_downsample_hr(predicted_2, n = n_downsample)
  out$iteration <- i
  out
})

downsample_hrs <- do.call(rbind, downsample_hrs)


downsample_summary <- data.frame(
  mean_hr = mean(downsample_hrs$hr, na.rm = TRUE),
  median_hr = median(downsample_hrs$hr, na.rm = TRUE),
  lower_hr = quantile(downsample_hrs$hr, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
  upper_hr = quantile(downsample_hrs$hr, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),
  mean_log_hr = mean(downsample_hrs$log_hr, na.rm = TRUE),
  sd_log_hr = sd(downsample_hrs$log_hr, na.rm = TRUE),
  proportion_significant = mean(downsample_hrs$p_value < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
  proportion_protective = mean(downsample_hrs$hr < 1, na.rm = TRUE),
  mean_events = mean(downsample_hrs$events, na.rm = TRUE)
)

downsample_summary
```

###  Create table

```{r }
# Full Class 2 Cox model -------------------------------------------------------

predicted_2$tx <- factor(predicted_2$tx, levels = c("control", "rapa"))

full_fit <- coxph(
  Surv(le_wk, dead_censor) ~ tx,
  data = predicted_2
)

full_summary <- summary(full_fit)

full_hr <- full_summary$conf.int["txrapa", "exp(coef)"]
full_lower <- full_summary$conf.int["txrapa", "lower .95"]
full_upper <- full_summary$conf.int["txrapa", "upper .95"]
full_p <- full_summary$coefficients["txrapa", "Pr(>|z|)"]

full_n <- nrow(predicted_2)
full_events <- sum(predicted_2$dead_censor == 1, na.rm = TRUE)


# Downsampled sensitivity summary --------------------------------------------

downsample_n <- unique(downsample_hrs$n)

if (length(downsample_n) != 1) {
  downsample_n <- 85
}

down_median_hr <- median(downsample_hrs$hr, na.rm = TRUE)
down_lower_hr <- quantile(downsample_hrs$hr, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE)
down_upper_hr <- quantile(downsample_hrs$hr, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE)

prop_protective <- mean(downsample_hrs$hr < 1, na.rm = TRUE)
prop_significant <- mean(downsample_hrs$p_value < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)


# Helper formatting functions -------------------------------------------------

fmt_num <- function(x, digits = 2) {
  formatC(as.numeric(x), format = "f", digits = digits)
}

fmt_p <- function(p) {
  ifelse(
    is.na(p),
    "",
    ifelse(p < 0.001, "<0.001", formatC(p, format = "f", digits = 3))
  )
}

fmt_pct <- function(x) {
  paste0(round(100 * x), "%")
}


# Manuscript table ------------------------------------------------------------

hr_table <- data.frame(
  Analysis = c(
    "Full Class 2 cohort",
    "Class 2 downsampled sensitivity analysis"
  ),
  `Sample size` = c(
    paste0(full_n, " animals; ", full_events, " events"),
    paste0(downsample_n, " animals per resample")
  ),
  `HR` = c(
    fmt_num(full_hr, 2),
    paste0("Median ", fmt_num(down_median_hr, 2))
  ),
  `Interval` = c(
    paste0("95% CI: ", fmt_num(full_lower, 2), "–", fmt_num(full_upper, 2)),
    paste0(
      "Empirical 2.5th–97.5th percentile: ",
      fmt_num(down_lower_hr, 2), "–", fmt_num(down_upper_hr, 2)
    )
  ),
  `p-value / robustness` = c(
    paste0("p = ", fmt_p(full_p)),
    paste0(
      "HR < 1 in ", fmt_pct(prop_protective),
      "; p < 0.05 in ", fmt_pct(prop_significant)
    )
  ),
  check.names = FALSE
)


ft <- flextable(hr_table)

ft <- theme_booktabs(ft)
ft <- autofit(ft)

ft <- align(ft, align = "left", part = "all")
ft <- bold(ft, part = "header")
ft <- fontsize(ft, size = 10, part = "all")
ft <- fontsize(ft, size = 9, part = "footer")

ft <- add_header_lines(
  ft,
  values = "Class 2 Rapamycin Survival Sensitivity Analysis"
)

# Lettered footnotes on the column headers. add_header_lines() above put the
# title in header row 1, so the column names are row 2 -- that is what i = 2
# targets. Order here sets the a/b/c order in the footer.
ft <- flextable::footnote(
  ft,
  i = 2, j = "HR", part = "header",
  value = as_paragraph("HR reflects rapamycin vs. control."),
  ref_symbols = "a"
)

ft <- flextable::footnote(
  ft,
  i = 2, j = "Sample size", part = "header",
  value = as_paragraph(
    "Downsampled estimates are based on repeated random sampling of Class 2 to n = 85."
  ),
  ref_symbols = "b"
)

ft <- flextable::footnote(
  ft,
  i = 2, j = "Interval", part = "header",
  value = as_paragraph(
    "Empirical 2.5-97.5th percentile range covers the middle 95% of the distribution."
  ),
  ref_symbols = "c"
)

ft <- bold(ft, i = 1, part = "header")
ft <- align(ft, i = 1, align = "center", part = "header")

ft

save_as_image(
  ft,
  path = file.path("output", "tables", "class2_downsampled_hr_table.png"),
  zoom = 2
)
```


```{r hist HRs}
# Figure 5J. Axis labels are the published ones and are set HERE, not in
# 99_pub_ready_figs: this is base graphics, so there is no ggplot object for a
# later stage to restyle -- hist() draws straight to the device.
#
# The y axis is a PERCENTAGE of the downsampled replicates, not a count, so the
# counts are converted before plotting. Labelling the raw counts "Frequency (%)"
# would have misstated the axis.
hr_hist <- hist(downsample_hrs$hr, breaks = 40, plot = FALSE)
hr_hist$counts <- 100 * hr_hist$counts / sum(hr_hist$counts)

hist_args <- list(
  hr_hist,
  main = "Downsampled Class 2 Treatment HRs",
  xlab = "Hazard ratio for rapa vs. control",
  ylab = "Frequency (%)"
)

do.call(plot, hist_args)

hist_out <- file.path("output", "downsampled_hr_histogram")
dir.create(hist_out, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
png(
  # square: this is figure 5J and sits beside square panels
  filename = file.path(hist_out, "downsampled_class2_hr_histogram.png"),
  width = 6,
  height = 6,
  units = "in",
  res = 300,
  bg = "white"
)

do.call(plot, hist_args)

abline(v = 1, lty = 2, lwd = 2)
abline(v = median(downsample_hrs$hr, na.rm = TRUE), lwd = 2)

dev.off()
```

```{r save predicted csv}
write.csv(predicted, file = "output/treatment_class.csv")
```
